entry.title: Currency Mismatch in “Make Bank Transfer” App – House Bank Currency USD but System Showing GBP
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have created a Bank Key (Region – GB) and a House Bank, with the bank location set to London and the bank account currency maintained as USD. The bank key region is also maintained as “GB.” For reference, the company code currency is IQD.

However, when we perform a bank-to-bank transfer using the “Make Bank Transfer” app, the system displays the currency as GBP instead of USD, which differs from the currency maintained during the bank master creation.

Could you please confirm whether this is standard system behavior, or if there is any configuration we might be missing?

Any guidance on this would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you

Rohini Dutta

 

Screenshot for reference

entry.title: Re: Common Inventory Audit Challenges During Stock Taking
entry.content:
Below are the Best Practices to minimize discrepancies

1) Pre-audit preparation
Freeze movements in audited locations during counting. Clean up master data (storage bins, material statuses).

2) Structured counting process
Use counting teams with segregation of duties (one counts, another verifies). Provide clear instructions and training to counters.

3) Technology support
Use barcode scanners or mobile devices to reduce manual entry errors. Leverage SAP’s cycle counting for high-value or fast-moving items.

4) Handling special stock
Define procedures for damaged, obsolete, or consignment stock. Record stock in transit separately and reconcile after audit.

5) Post-count reconciliation
Investigate large variances immediately. Document reasons for differences (e.g., shrinkage, misplacement).

Some SAP-specific tips are

  • Use the Physical Inventory document in SAP MM to control and track counts.
  • Apply inventory sampling (if allowed by auditors) to reduce workload.
  • Leverage SAP reports (e.g., MI20 for differences, MI24 for list of inventory documents).
  • Use batch management to handle expired/damaged stock more accurately.
  • Consider cycle counting in SAP (transaction MICN) for continuous accuracy.

Hi dear friends, 

I have a situation here., we are in actual costing scenario.

Basically,  some GL accounts (cost elements) are moved from cost component SemiFinish (CC A)  to cost component By-Product (CC B)

But the subsequent operations work still on CCS A.

3012656 - How to change cost component structures in S/4 HANA

talks about cost component change, so replacing the CCS with something else altogether. I feel this is not my case. 

the mentioned reports /FCML4H_CCS_CHANGE and find nothing FCML4H_CCS_RESET. That is probably fair, because amount of cost components does not change. 

So.  Question please. What is the procedure to move a GL account from CC A to CC B? 

thank you.

Regards,
Laimonas

entry.title: Re: MR8M values different from invoice values
entry.content:
When you reverse an invoice with MR8M, the system recalculates values in both company code currency (local currency) and document currency (hard currency). It’s quite common to see differences if:

  • Exchange rates have changed between the original posting and the reversal.
  • The invoice had tax or conditions calculated differently at reversal time.
  • The system is using current exchange rate tables rather than the rate at the time of the original invoice.
  • Rounding differences occur between currencies.

In short, the mismatch is usually due to exchange rate or tax recalculation. The recommended approach is to analyze the currency settings and exchange rate usage, not to override with manual entries.

entry.title: Personas script - Prevent users to reach Main Screen
entry.content:
HI all

I’m facing an issue with a Personas setup and I’m not sure how to solve it.

We have a direct URL for Personas that takes the user straight into a specific flavor and transaction code (/SCWM/RFUI). We are not using roles to launch this flavor, because our user base changes constantly. Instead, we simply provide the direct URL on the handheld device so we don’t need to maintain users in a specific role.

The problem is:
When the user presses Back (F7), they are taken to the main initial screen. We want to avoid this for two reasons:

  1. The URL should make the process extremely simple — users should only access this flavor and tcode, nothing else.
  2. When the user refreshes the URL after returning to the main screen, they receive an error. After that, the screen becomes completely stuck and the user needs to fully close the Chrome app and restart everything, which is a terrible experience.

I tried using the onFinalize event:

if (session.info.program === "SAPLSMTR_NAVIGATION") {
    session.utils.alert("Main screen");
}

During debugging, I noticed that all flavor-level events are triggered before the main screen is fully loaded, so this approach won’t work.I also considered creating a flavor for the main screen and redirecting back to /SCWM/RFUI, but that would require maintaining the user in /personas/admin, which we want to avoid.

Finally, I thought about intercepting the F7 key, but that would affect all other screens where F7 needs to behave normally as a Back function.

Does anyone have an idea on how to solve this?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Керівну директорку SAP Ukraine номіновано до рейтингу «ТОП-50 лідерів українського бізнесу»
entry.content:
Друзі, маємо чудову новину для нашої SAP-спільноти!✨

Ганну Мікулицьку, керівну директорку SAP Ukraine, номіновано до рейтингу «ТОП-50 лідерів українського бізнесу» 2026 року 🙌
На сайті delo.ua триває голосування за найкращих керівників українських компаній. За його результатами експертне журі та редакція визначать тих, хто створює нову економіку країни.

У своїй роботі Ганна керується принципами, які сьогодні формують нову якість лідерства:
Прозорість і відповідальність, сміливість, а також людяність.
Як лідер, вона системно розвиває SAP-екосистему в Україні: сприяє цифровій трансформації бізнесу, розвиває партнерську мережу, активно підтримує державні ініціативи, спрямовані на розвиток IT-індустрії та зміцнення економіки країни.

Особливу увагу вона приділяє зростанню й розвитку локальної команди, створенню мотивуючого середовища, а також забезпеченню безпеки, добробуту та турботи про здоров’я співробітників.

Ганна не лише допомагає у трансформації бізнесу, а й підсилює голос України на глобальній арені.
Підтримайте Ганну своїм голосом💙💛
Голосування триває до 20 числа включно.

Проголосувати можна тут:
https://delo.ua/business/startuvalo-golosuvannya-reitingu-top-50-lideriv-ukrayinskogo-biznesu-2026-…

entry.title: Launch of the Configuring SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors Learning Journey: Your Fast Track
entry.content:
Connecting SmartRecruiters with SAP SuccessFactors is a powerful step toward streamlined hiring—but the real value comes when teams know how to use that connection consistently, correctly, and at scale. That’s why we’re excited to announce the launch of the Configuring SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors Learning Journey, now available on SAP Learning.

This Learning Journey is designed to help implementation partners, delivery teams, customers, and project teams accelerate adoption throughout their implementation journey.

Explore the Learning Journey:

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/configuring-smartrecruiters-for-sap-successfactors

Why this Learning Journey?

Teams implementing or expanding SmartRecruiters alongside SAP SuccessFactors often face the same challenge: knowledge is distributed across a few experts, documentation is scattered, and new team members take time to ramp.

The Configuring SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors Learning Journey provides a course plus a certification to help with the following:

  • Reduce ramp-up time for new consultants, admins and project members
  • Create shared understanding across project implementation teams
  • Ensure process consistency and data integrity
  • Mitigate implementation risks and ensure compliance

What you’ll learn (course highlights)

The SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors Academy (part of this Learning Journey) is built to give learners a clear understanding of the joint SmartRecruiters + SAP SuccessFactors landscape, with content that includes:

  • Introducing SmartRecruiters
  • Exploring the SmartRecruiters System
  • Configuring Company Admin Settings
  • Managing Users and Roles for Recruiting
  • Configuring Jobs
  • Advertising Jobs and Sourcing
  • Configuring the Candidate Experience
  • Configuring Interview Settings
  • Configuring the Hiring Process
  • Managing the Offer Process
  • Managing Communications and Notifications
  • Exploring Winston Chat and Winston Match
  • Exploring Candidate Relationship Management
  • Implementing Global Compliance

Who it’s for?

This Academy and the certification are valuable for anyone involved in implementation and delivery, admins and project teams within the SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors ecosystem. These include:

  • Implementation teams and partners
  • HR Admins
  • Project Implementation teams

How to use this Learning Journey in your rollout (recommended)

To get the most from the learning, we recommend a simple adoption approach:

  1. Encourage completion of the Learning Journey early (project kickoff / design phase) to align teams on core concepts.
  2. Use it as onboarding for new project members and admins joining midstream
  3. Keep it as a reference for ongoing optimization and continuous improvement
  4. After completing the Academy, take the accompanying certification.

Get started today

The Configuring SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors Learning Journey is live now on SAP Learning:

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/configuring-smartrecruiters-for-sap-successfactors

Ready, Set, Learn!

entry.title: SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and Joule health checks
entry.content:
As a UX specialist in the SAP S/4HANA Regional Implementation Group, my team and I often get to work with customers keen to leverage the significant capabilities of SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and Joule.

With so much interest in Joule and so many use cases available, our aim is to make it easier for you to configure your use cases efficiently and support your business users as they start to use Joule every day.

Good news! In this blog we cover simple checks to ensure that your SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, and Joule architecture is correctly configured and working properly before you onboard your business users.

Performing these checks will allow you to become more confident and leverage the significant benefits of Joule.

NOTE 1: There are 2 different build plans for SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition. Further details on the foundation and standard plans can be found here: SAP Note: 3704047 - Comparison of Joule functionalities while using SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition using foundation plan or standard plan. This blog is based on SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, standard plan. Troubleshooting options may differ between the foundation and standard build plans.

NOTE 2: The simple tests detailed here are intended to be run AFTER SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and Joule have been fully configured.

 

Architecture

Below, you can see the relationship of SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, Joule, SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP), SAP Cloud Identity Service, (CIS), SAP Identity Authentication Services (IAS) and SAP Identity Provisioning Services (IPS) and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.

Understanding this architecture is critical to testing and troubleshooting your landscape!

Prerequisites

Make sure that you have met all prerequisites before you expose content to your launchpad. It is essential that SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and Joule have been configured correctly as per the following:

  • SAP Help: Joule Onboarding Guide

  • SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, standard edition - integration with Joule

  • Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide

  • SAP Note: 3523238 - Navigational and Transactional capabilities with Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

1.    Introduction

You can test if Joule has been technically correctly configured by prompting Joule to fetch information from the backend system, e.g. ‘Display Sales Order 911’ as in the example below:

Sample Joule capability

Based on the reply from Joule, you can proceed as follows:

- If Joule provides answers with specific sales orders -> the technical configuration is fine

- If Joule does not provide answers, this could have multiple causes:

  • Principal Propagation is not working

  • SAP Identity Provisioning service jobs have not run correctly or have run with errors

  • SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition or content exposure is not correct

  • Timeouts in the backend

  • Role assignment is not correct

  • Other errors

In the following sections we will go through the steps associated with checking the above in more detail.

 

2. Principal Propagation

Principal Propagation is the authentication mechanism that SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, Joule use to authenticate users, and it is essential that it is configured properly. You can check if your Principal Propagation configuration is correct as follows:

  • Test from Joule using a transactional capability as mentioned in the Introduction section of this blog
  • In case you have or can easily add an SAP Business Application Studio instance to the Joule subaccount, test the  "jouledt" and "joulert" destinations with Principal Propagation configured using curl command as described in this blog : How to check the connectivity to your backend system in Business Application Studio
  • Or, from a browser, you can simulate a Principal Propagation request also with the “foundation” plan of SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition.

           - Simulate a Principal Propagation request also with the foundation plan of SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition
           - Identify any OData service in the system that provides data (e.g. by calling a Fiori Elements application and check the                   Developer Tools & copy the full value of the URL (e.g. into Notepad)
           - Copy the existing “rt” destination and change its values to use so-called “Tunnelled Access” via the SAP Cloud                                 - Connector (see DT destination for hostname and additional parameters). Configure “Principal Propagation” as                                    the Authentication mechanism
            - Based on the OData service and destination, you can construct the URL of the OData service how it is called passing                      Work Zone and Cloud Connector https://<workzoneTenant>.sapdelim-<destination>.<workzoneDomain>/<ODataURL>

       Principal Propagation troubleshooting tools are as follows:

       (a) SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition transactions:

                - SMICM (use trace level 3 to capture most detail)

                - CERTRULE (check rule-based mappings)

                Refer to SAP Note: 2351619 - How to take SMICM trace?

         (b) SAP Cloud Connector logs

Refer to How to troubleshoot Cloud Connector principal propagation over HTTPS

NOTE: We are currently updating our Principal Propagation enablement content, check back here for details!

 

3. SAP Cloud Connector

When you are testing Principal Propagation (in #2), you could consider to temporarily turn on SAP Cloud Connector logs (at a suitably appropriate time of low system usage) which will detect errors such as:

  • Principal Propagation and authentication issues

  • Backend server connectivity issues (e.g., incorrect hostname or protocol, expired certificates)

  • Service issues (e.g., inactive or not found)

In the example below, you can see that authentication has failed due to an expired certificate.

SAP Cloud Connector - expired certificate

 If an error does not show up as expected, you can consider increasing the trace level of the logs as shown below. For SSL issues, a TLS trace has to be active, and for other issues, you need to look at the tunnel trace.

SAP Cloud Connector - trace levels

You should also turn on SAP Cloud Connector Alerting so you can be kept informed about critical system issues (expiring certificates, disk space, CPU load etc…) BEFORE THEY OCCUR.

THIS IS ESPECIALLY IMPORTANT IN YOUR PRODUCTION LANDSCAPE!!!!!

EXAMPLE: By turning on Alerts for expiring certificates, an email alert would be sent 30 days before a certificate expires, so a new certificate can be generated and used, thereby preventing any connectivity issues:

Refer to SAP BTP Connectivity - Alerting

 

4. Check your content exposure

You have already run transaction code /UI2/CDM3_EXP_SCOPE to expose your chosen roles to SAP BTP. Refer to:

  • SAP Help: Manage Launchpad Content for Exposure

  • Blog: Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide

Now, you can check if your content has been successfully exposed and is ready for consumption by SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP).

Exposure is available in two versions: the current Version 1 and the new Version 2 as follows:

  • Version 1 is based on legacy target mappings

  • Version 2 provides a seamless end-to-end app model, extending from the SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library to business user usage. Version 2 is based on Launchpad App Descriptor Items (LADIs) and is the long-term, future-proof successor to Version 1.

NOTE: Since apps have different IDs in version 2, there is no compatible upgrade path from version 1 to version 2. Implementing integration using version 2 requires initiating a new project.

Depending on whether you are using Version 1 or Version 2, the following URLs can be used to test your content exposure to SAP BTP:

  • Version 1 http://<your-server-name>:<your-port-number>/sap/bc/ui2/cdm3/entities

  • Version 2 http://<your-server-name>:<your-port-number>/sap/bc/http/ui2/flp_content_exposure/entities

Version 1 and 2 have different URLs, 1 is based on classical tiles and target mappings and 2 is based on LADIs.

The URLs must be run in the browser (not via SAP BTP) and expose content in JSON format. Below, you can see that SAP Fiori App F0233A using semantic object ‘OutboundDelivery’ and action ‘displayFactSheet’ has successfully been exposed from a backend SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition system and can  be read by SAP BTP.

Test the url directly in a  browser (not via SAP BTP) with the system user configured in the design time destination (JOULE_ADMIN in the configuration guide).

Testing content exposureRefer to:

  • SAP Note: 3501486 - Launchpad Content Exposed with version 2 not updated in SAP Build Workzone, Standard edition

  • SAP Note: 3345119 - Expose Launchpad Content to SAP BTP - Exposure Version 2

  • SAP Help: Manage Launchpad Content for Exposure

 

5. SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition

Now you can check your Content Channel, and the associated destinations work correctly

  • Check that the Design Time and Runtime destinations (‘jouledt’ and ‘joulert’) work successfully:Destination TestsExample: An incorrect Design Time destination will cause errors importing roles from SAP S/4HANA Cloud  Private Edition. NOTE: The Ping does *not* fully check the entire destination. If it is “red” you know something is wrong, but “green” does not mean that everything is fine

  • You can check if your Content Channel synchronization works successfully (NOTE: the synchronization will not work if  the destination pings in (a) have not been successfully executed). Click on the ‘Synch’ button to consume content exposed from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition:

  • Click on the ‘Report’ link to check that there are no synch errors:

  •  Check the Content Explorer to ensure that content exposed through a channel has been successfully consumed by SAP BTP- From the Site Manager page, select Content Manager and then Content Explorer:

  • Chose the Channel through which your content was consumed by SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition:

  • Here you can see the content consumed through your selected channel. In this example, you can see that 31 roles have been exposed from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and consumed by SAP BTP.   

  • Check roles exposed from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition have been successfully consumed by SAP BTP:

  • Check that your test user has the correctly exposed roles assigned:

        NOTE: If you do not see any roles here, check the following:

  • The test user has the necessary roles in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

  • Roles have been exposed via Content Exposure

  • SAP Identity Provisioning service jobs have run and completed successfully

Refer to:

  • SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, standard edition - troubleshooting

  • Integration Guide: SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, Standard Edition Integration with SAP S/4HANA

 

5. Identity Provisioning service

The navigation service of SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition uses the SAP Identity Provisioning service (IPS). IPS provisions identities and their authorizations between source and target systems. To use the navigational capabilities in Joule, you need to configure SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition as the source and SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, standard edition as the target system in IPS. Here, you confirm successful provisioning by reviewing logs and status of synchronizing jobs.

- In SAP Cloud Identity Services, view Identity Provisioning -> Provisioning Logs:

Provisioning logs

- Now you can see the Job Execution Logs and check them to ensure there are no errors:

Execution logs

 - In this example, you can see that user BPINST cannot be provisioned correctly by SAP IPS as it has NO email address -> this  user will experience issues with SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and Joule.

Example execution log

6.    Joule Welcome

Once all the above tests have been successfully executed, you can run Joule. If Joule has been configured correctly then there will be a welcome screen with Joule skills showing as in the example below:

Joule debugger console

Click on the debug icon shown above to check there are no errors in the debugger console:

Now you can go ahead and start using Joule and leverage its business value!

 

7.Help! I have tried all the above checks and still have problems

Don’t worry! There are lots of great troubleshooting tools.

a)  Check if the related Fiori apps on the SAP S/4HANA Fiori Launchpad are working without issues:

      App Support: Troubleshoot configuration and authorization errors for apps in the SAP Fiori launchpad.

     Refer to:

  • App Support
  • Setting Up App Support

       App Inspector: Troubleshoot configuration and authorization errors for apps available through the SAP Fiori Launchpad

  Refer to:

  • App Inspector
  • Setting Up App Inspector

b) In Joule, you have the Debug button (click on the ‘Debug’ button show below):

  • Highlights service issues and general errors
  • Active conversation with Joule
  • Response Payload: Shows aggregated JSON response for each Joule interaction
  • Requests Logs: Shows requests and responses grouped by prompt

Joule debug console

 c)  SAP Cloud Connector Logs. These logs can have the following use cases:

  • Principal propagation and authentication troubleshooting
  • Backend server connectivity (e.g., incorrect hostname or protocol, expired certificates)
  • Service issues (e.g., inactive or not found)

 The logs can be accessed in the SAP Cloud Connector as shown:

SAP Cloud Connector logs

Refer to SAP Help: Cloud Connector Trace and Logging

d) SAP Gateway Error Log

  • Transaction /IWFND/ERROR_LOG
  • Shows service errors (e.g., forbidden, not found, inconsistent calls)

      SAP Gateway Tracing Tools

  • Transaction /IWFND/TRACES
  • Troubleshoot service issues, capture header and payload data

      Internet Communication Manager Monitor

  • Transaction SMICM
  • Detailed HTTP calls (by increasing trace level to level 3)
  • Helps to identify authentication and SSL issues

       Refer to SAP Help: Gateway Tracing Tools

Still stuck? Refer to SAP Help: Joule - Monitoring and Troubleshooting which includes details on raising SAP incidents.

Troubleshooting:

  • Blog: Common configuration issues: Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • SAP Help: SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, standard edition - Troubleshooting
  • SAP Help: How to troubleshoot Cloud Connector principal propagation over HTTPS
  • SAP Help: Joule Constraints
  • SAP Note: 3649670 - Missing Joule icon in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition Standard Edition
  • SAP Note: 3666014 - Information required for Joule
  • SAP Note: 3673511 - Troubleshooting for Joule page display issues
  • SAP Note: 3679363 - How to recognize a Joule response's

Becoming an SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA guru

You’ll find much more on the community topic page for SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

Other helpful links in the SAP Community:

  • Follow our tag SAP S/4HANA RIG for more from the SAP S/4HANA Customer Care and RIG
  • See all questions and answers about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA
  • Follow SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA for more blogs and updates
  • Ask a Question about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Re: Hello SAP Community! My name is Adithya Ajith and I’m excited to begin my journey here
entry.content:
@adithya_ajith - Thanks for your contribution. It's refreshing to see such a human-oriented set of ambitions. People like you will be critical in steering innovation in a way that serves people instead of just profit margins.

My hope is AI playing a role in reestablishing some sort of objectivity in the information that people are consuming. Any assistance in undoing the tribal and divisive nature of social media algorithms will be important in the next months and years.

entry.title: Royalties Settlement - Additional Business Volume Table Requirement Class
entry.content:
Dear Experts,
 
We are implementing Royalties Settlement within SAP Condition Contract Management (best practice scope item: 55I)
 
The business requirement is for royalty rebate business volume to reference Royalty Only Paid Invoices from SAP standard business volume table: V_WB2_VBRK_VBRP_4P and the delta accruals business volume to reference the sales order line-item net value of the rebate associated unpaid and paid billing documents. Both rebate and accruals will use the same business volume field selection which is material and utilise same value scales %. Also, the business volume determination will not be using Royalty Recipient partner data as a reference in the billing documents.
 
My proposed configuration approach for the accrual BVB determination:
  1. Create a new Business Volume table for sales order and item data or extend an existing CDS view of unpaid and paid billing docs to include the billed items' assigned sales order item value to capture the accruals business volume amount.
  2. Create a new condition type to capture the accruals' business volume and assign it to the Delta Accruals pricing procedure.
  3. Assign Amount Fields to the accrual BVB condition type and business fieldname in the detailed statement
  4. Set up a selection group and assign to relevant new BVB table.
 
My query is understanding what the Requirements Class is used for in the Business Volume Determination profile configuration. I noticed there is an SAP standard Requirement Class: CL_WB2_BVTAB_REQ_NO_LIFRO_DA that lists Without Royalties Recipient + Delta Accruals in its description, which seems fitting for this requirement. Therefore, for the above delta accrual business volume determination, should this requirement class be assigned in the Business Volume Determination Profile under the sub folder: Additional Business Volumes Tables config to the new sales order item business volume table? For further understanding, this particular requirements class is applicable for delta accrual business volume calculation only and therefore will not impact the royalty rebate business volume calculation?
 
Appreciate your feedback.
 
Many thanks,
Paul

entry.title: Triggering SAP Databricks Jobs through SAP Datasphere Task Chains
entry.content:
Abstract

Integrating SAP Datasphere with Databricks opens up powerful possibilities for advanced analytics, machine learning pipelines, and cross-platform data processing.

In this guide, I'll share my experience automating SAP Databricks notebook execution using SAP Datasphere task chains and the Databricks Jobs API through a practical, step-by-step approach.

Common Use Cases:

  • Automated data processing pipelines across SAP and Databricks
  • Machine learning model training triggered by Datasphere data flows
  • Advanced analytics on SAP data using Databricks' compute power
  • Orchestrated cross-platform workflows with unified monitoring

Business Value:

  • Reduced manual intervention in data workflows
  • Faster time-to-insight with automated analytics
  • Unified orchestration across enterprise data platforms

Prerequisites

Before getting started, ensure you have:

  • SAP Datasphere access with permission to create connections and task chains
  • Databricks workspace with notebook development access
  • Databricks Personal Access Token (PAT) generation privileges
  • Basic understanding of REST APIs and HTTP methods
  • A Databricks notebook ready to be executed

Architecture Overview

The integration follows this flow:

┌─────────────────────┐
│  SAP Datasphere     │
│  Task Chain         │
└──────────┬──────────┘
           │
           │ HTTP POST Request
           │ (Jobs API)
           ▼
┌─────────────────────┐
│  Databricks         │
│  Jobs API           │
└──────────┬──────────┘
           │
           │ Triggers
           ▼
┌─────────────────────┐
│  Databricks         │
│  Notebook Run       │
└─────────────────────┘

Key Components:

  1. Datasphere Task Chain - Orchestration layer
  2. Generic HTTP Connection - API authentication setup
  3. Databricks Jobs API - /api/2.1/jobs/runs/submit endpoint
  4. PAT Authentication - Bearer token security
  5. Databricks Notebook - Target execution logic

Step 1: Create a Generic HTTP Connection in Datasphere

The first step is to establish a secure connection from Datasphere to Databricks. We'll use a Generic HTTP Connection with Bearer token authentication.

Navigate to Connections

In SAP Datasphere, go to Connections and create a new Generic HTTP connection.

Configure Connection Settings

Setting Value Notes

Connection Namedatabricks_rest_apiChoose a descriptive name
Host<your-workspace>.cloud.databricks.comYour Databricks workspace hostname
Port443Datasphere uses 443 (HTTPS) as default
ProtocolHTTPSAlways use secure connections
Path/api/2.1 (optional)Can be set here or in the API task later

Authentication Setup

Databricks uses Bearer tokens (Personal Access Tokens / PATs) for API authentication. For simplicity in Datasphere, we'll configure this as a Username/Password authentication type:

Setting Value

Authentication TypeUsername and Password
Usernametoken
Password<Your Databricks PAT>

Note: The username is literally the string token. This is how Databricks interprets Bearer token authentication via basic auth.

Obtaining a Databricks Personal Access Token (PAT)

You'll need a PAT to authenticate your API calls. Here's how to generate one:

  1. In your Databricks workspace, click on your user profile in the top-right corner
  2. Select Settings
  3. Navigate to Developer under the User section
  4. Click on Manage next to Access Tokens

  1. Click Generate new token
  2. Provide a description (e.g., "Datasphere Task Chain Integration")
  3. Set an expiration period (recommended: 90 days for security)
  4. Copy the generated token immediately as you won't be able to see it again!

Test Your Connection

Before proceeding, verify that your PAT is working correctly by making a test API call:

curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <your-PAT>" \
  https://<your-workspace>.cloud.databricks.com/api/2.1/jobs/list

If configured correctly, you should receive a JSON response listing your Databricks jobs (or an empty list if you have none as in my case).

Step 2: Create a Datasphere Task Chain

Now that the connection is established, let's create a task chain that will trigger the Databricks notebook.

Create a New Task Chain

  1. In Datasphere, navigate to Data Builder
  2. Create a new Task Chain
  3. Give it a descriptive name (e.g., trigger_databricks_notebook)

Add an API Task

From the task palette, drag the API task onto the canvas.

Step 3: Configure the API Task

This is where we define how Datasphere will call the Databricks Jobs API.

Basic Configuration

Click on the API task to open its configuration panel.

Setting Value

Connectiondatabricks_rest_api (the connection you created earlier)
HTTP MethodPOST

API Endpoint Configuration

The endpoint depends on how you configured your connection:

Setting Value

Full Endpoint (if Path is empty in connection)/api/2.1/jobs/runs/submit
Partial Endpoint (if Path is /api/2.1 in connection)/jobs/runs/submit

This endpoint is used to submit a one-off notebook run without needing a pre-configured Databricks job. For more details, see the Databricks Jobs API documentation.

Request Body

The request body defines what notebook to run and with what parameters. Here's what I used:

{
    "name": "<job-name>",
    "tasks": [{
        "task_key": "run_notebook",
        "notebook_task": {
            "notebook_path": "/Workspace/Users/<your-user>/<your-notebook>"
        },
        "environment_key": "Default",
        "timeout_seconds": 3600
    }],
    "environments": [{
        "environment_key": "Default",
        "spec": {
            "client": "1"
        }
    }],
    "max_concurrent_runs": 1
}

Request Body Breakdown

Field Purpose

nameA descriptive name for this job run (appears in Databricks UI)
tasks[].task_keyUnique identifier for this task within the job
tasks[].notebook_task.notebook_pathFull path to your Databricks notebook
tasks[].environment_keyReferences the environment configuration
tasks[].timeout_secondsMaximum execution time (3600 = 1 hour)
environments[].environment_keyEnvironment identifier (must match task's reference)
environments[].spec.clientClient specification for the environment
max_concurrent_runsHow many instances of this job can run simultaneously

Serverless Compute Note: The example above uses Databricks Serverless Compute, so no cluster specification is required. If you're using a dedicated cluster, you'll need to add a new_cluster or existing_cluster_id field in the task definition.

Response Configuration

For monitoring purposes, configure the response handling:

Setting Value

Response TypeHTTP Status Code
Success Status Codes200, 201

The API will return a 200 OK status if the notebook run was successfully triggered, along with a JSON response containing the run_id.

 

Step 4: Test Your Task Chain

Before deploying to production, thoroughly test your integration.

Execute the Task Chain

  1. Save your task chain configuration
  2. Click Run to execute the task chain manually
  3. Monitor the execution in the Datasphere task chain log

Verify in Databricks

  1. Navigate to your Databricks workspace
  2. Go to Jobs & Pipelines
  3. You should see a new run with the name datasphere_job (or whatever you specified)
  4. Click on the run to see execution details and logs

entry.title: “Object reference not set to an instance of an object” when previewing Crystal Reports Layouts
entry.content:
Hi all,

I’m facing an issue with SAP Business One related to Crystal Reports layouts.

When trying to preview any layout, I receive the following error:

Object reference not set to an instance of an object.

This issue occurs across all layouts and all databases on the main server where SAP B1 is installed.

However, I have a separate RDS server running SAP B1 Client where the same layouts work perfectly without any issues. So the problem seems specific to the main SAP server environment.

Additionally, when using the SAP tool:
“Preview External Crystal Reports File”, I get: Error while running Crystal report

Environment:

SAP B1 Version: SAP Business One 10.0 (10.00.300) FP 2508 (64-bit)

Crystal Reports Version: SAP Crystal Reports 14.3.4.4938

What I’ve tried so far:

  • Uninstalled and reinstalled Crystal Reports
  • Reinstalled CRRuntime
  • Verified that layouts preview correctly directly inside Crystal Reports (no issues there)

If anyone has encountered this issue or has suggestions, your help would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Issue During SAP SuccessFactors AI Role Certification Exam – Stuck on Scoring Screen
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to report a technical issue I experienced during my SAP SuccessFactors AI Role Certification exam.

After completing all questions, I submitted my answers and proceeded to the scoring phase. However, the system became stuck on the scoring/loading screen and did not progress for an extended period of time. Despite waiting, the exam eventually ended with a “time up” status, and I was marked as failed.

I would like to clarify that I had already submitted all my responses and was in the scoring stage when this issue occurred. Therefore, this appears to be a system-related problem rather than an issue with my exam attempt.

I have already submitted a support ticket through SAP Learning, and I also have a backup file of my responses.

Has anyone experienced a similar issue, or is there any additional action I should take to ensure this is properly reviewed?

Thank you in advance for your help.

entry.content:
When intercepting the F7 key, try to check if a certain control is currently on the screen. For example, look for the control ID of the warehouse number input field, and only continue with suppressing the F7 action if it's present.

If this is not acceptable (because the same control ID is also on other screens), then try finding something else that is unique to the initial screen.

entry.title: How does delta works for Datasphere Transformation Flow?
entry.content:
Hello,

At the moment, I do not have sufficient time to conduct a proof of concept (POC) in our DSP system, as I am currently engaged with other priorities. However, in preparation for future DSP-related tasks, I would like to understand how delta handling works in a Transformation Flow.

Scenario:
We have three Local Tables populated via Replication Flows:

  • LT1 – EKKO
  • LT2 – EKPO
  • LT3 – EKBE

These Local Tables are joined in a Graphical View based on defined business logic. The resulting View is then persisted (staged) into a new Local Table.

Question:
How does the delta mechanism behave for a Transformation Flow that extracts data from a View and loads it into a Local Table?

Note: I have not yet conducted a POC or performed detailed research on this topic.

entry.title: Re: Road map for SAP .Net connector for .Net core 10
entry.content:
Hi, 

We are aware of the fact that the next LTS version .NET 10 is available. Before this runtime can be supported a validation needs to happen that NCo works as expected with this runtime. This has not happened yet. The support strategy for NCo is described in SAP note https://me.sap.com/notes/2786882, and whenever it will be changed (adding new OS versions, .NET runtimes, dropping them, it will be updated accordingly). A concrete commitment is not possible in this forum. However, I can recommend to add the mentioned note as favorite note so that you get a notification once anything is changing with regard to the support strategy. 

Best regards,
Markus

entry.title: Re: Limit warehouse order for PI document
entry.content:
I understand this is an older thread, but I am providing this information in case others arrive here with the same query.

Unfortunately, it is not possible to use RDOCID as the field to limit warehouse order size, as previously suggested by Jurgen. This is because the actual physical inventory document is mapped elsewhere, meaning the RDOCID remains empty during the warehouse order limit calculation.

However, you can use the indicator "Do Not Split Warehouse Orders Between PI Documents" in SPRO -> EWM -> Master Data -> Define Warehouse Number Control:


*It might just be me, but I think the indicator's description is a bit misleading since we want to split the warehouse orders between physical inventory documents.

In some releases, the indicator could be missing from config so you would need to implement SAP note 3517096 to enable it.

Cheers,
Jon

entry.title: Teamwork Makes the AI Dream Work: Overview of Key Data Practitioner Roles
entry.content:
The transition to AI-driven business processes presents significant potential, but quality is directly influenced by the underlying data foundation. For smaller companies, the boundaries of a data foundation may be confined to a single database management system. Conversely, for larger enterprises, relevant data is more widely dispersed. Regardless of the size and scope, the expectations placed on the data team for this interconnected web of business context remain constant. Transactional data, master data, IoT data, unstructured data, multi-model data, and all other data types must be integrated into the IT landscape and optimized in a manner that facilitates multi-use and multi-purpose consumption.

A crucial factor in achieving success is the data platform's capacity to support the specific needs of each data professional while fulfilling the insatiable demands of artificial intelligence, analytics, and custom applications. As part of the launch of the data professional’s community, this wiki document along with the related blog posts offer resources that enable you, the data practitioner, to utilize SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC). As the #datapro community develops, resources will be available to help you optimally use the platform to address the data requirements of the business, IT systems, and the rest of your data ecosystem.

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) Overview

SAP Business Data Cloud is a SaaS cloud solution that connects data from SAP applications and external sources while preserving the business semantics necessary for trustworthy analytics and artificial intelligence. SAP BDC provides an intuitive foundation for data engineering, modeling, and governed sharing across regions and platforms. The solution supports both virtualization and replication, offers catalog and lineage for transparency, insightful analytics, and delivers the performance required for mixed workloads spanning structured and semi-structured data.

The portfolio includes core services and complementary capabilities such zero copy data sharing. The individual solutions include:

  • SAP Datasphere delivers a strategic business data fabric with semantic modeling, data virtualization and movement, cataloging, and controlled sharing.
  • SAP Analytics Cloud covers business intelligence, planning, and augmented analytics on curated models.
  • SAP Databricks integrates the Databricks Lakehouse with SAP semantics to streamline feature engineering, notebooks-driven exploration, and production machine learning pipelines.
  • SAP Business Warehouse supplies enterprise warehousing and transformation content that aligns with the cloud data fabric.
  • Intelligent Applications augment business processes using the data fabric and AI to adapt and learn from your data. With your business context, these applications can act on your behalf to transform business outcomes.
  • SAP Snowflake expands the data landscape by integrating its elastic platform with SAP business context for simultaneous analysis of SAP and non-SAP data at scale.
  • SAP Master Data Governance provides ready-to-run, domain-specific, master data management, so you can locally own and consolidate or centrally create, change, and distribute trusted master data across your enterprise system landscape.
  • SAP HANA Cloud provides a high-performance database and lake, supporting relational, document, graph, spatial, and time-series workloads with elastic scale. Note: SAP HANA Cloud is a BTP service but very relevant to data professionals and this community.

SAP BDC Blog Posts for Data Professionals 

For every team member to perform their best, access to the most relevant resources is a must. Consider the following set of curated content that could be relevant to your role on the data team.

Business Analyst

Business Analysts sit at the intersection of data and decision-making, translating complex information into insights that guide day-to-day operations and strategic initiatives. Their work depends on speed, consistency, and clarity, yet they are often slowed down by manual preparation, unclear definitions, or heavy reliance on technical teams.

 SAP Business Data Cloud equips Business Analysts with a unified suite of curated data products, intelligent applications, and planning and analytics tools, so they can move faster and act with confidence. Harmonized, contextual data stays consistent across domains while out-the-box dashboards and AI-driven insights are tailored to each line of business. Scenario modeling helps connect insights to decisions, and AI copilots like SAP Joule automate routine tasks so analysts can focus on higher-value work. The result: more agile, scalable analytics where insights don’t just inform decisions but actively drive them.

Visit this blog post for more insight on how BDC enables the Business Analyst to contribute to the success of the data and analytics team. 

Data Steward

A Data Steward is a business-savvy, analytical professional with deep expertise in data, particularly within a specific domain or line of business. Their role is to ensure the availability of well-governed data that is easily discoverable, reliable, up-to-date, and aligned with corporate and compliance standards.

SAP Business Data Cloud enhances this critical function by providing key features such as metadata management, including a centralized enterprise data catalog that tracks attributes, quality, lineage, and sensitivity. The catalog also enables governance and controlled access to data products. Additionally, for master data stewards, SAP Master Data Governance offers advanced capabilities for data quality management, consolidation, and centralized governance of business-critical master data domains.

Visit this blog post (coming soon) for more insight on how BDC enables the Data Steward to contribute to the success of the data and analytics team, along with the lines of business they support. 

Data Engineer

Data engineers facilitate the transfer of data from its source to its intended destinations. These data pipelines can become intricate, encompassing a substantial volume of data, a multitude of destinations, and complex logic. Upon the completion of data movement, this role actively participates in the development of the enterprise data model.

SAP Business Data Cloud streamlines data movement by serving as multiple roles, including a “control center” for data transfers. The act of centralization, coupled with data tiering, simplifies redundancy and complexity – resulting in the delivery of data in a timeframe necessary for a modern business process. Regardless of whether the data gravity is directed towards SAP Business Warehouse, SAP Datasphere, SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Databricks, SAP Snowflake, or any combination, data engineers assemble the business data fabric that fuels the company's applications, analytics, and artificial intelligence solutions.

Visit this blog post (coming soon) for more insight on how BDC enables the Data Engineer to contribute to the success of the data and analytics team. 

Data Modeler

Data modelers design and govern the business-semantic structures that make enterprise data consistent, reusable, and analytics ready. Day to day, they define entities, relationships, and keys; harmonize definitions across sources; document lineage and metadata; enforce data quality and security constraints; and evolve conceptual, logical, and physical models to support changing business needs.

SAP Business Data Cloud streamlines this work by providing a governed, business-semantic modeling layer in SAP Datasphere to define domains, entities, and relationships once and publish them as trusted data products. It connects to SAP and non-SAP sources with federation, centralizes glossary, lineage, and policy controls for impact analysis and access management, and integrates with platforms like Databricks and Snowflake so transformations can be pushed down and scaled without sacrificing governance.

Visit this blog post (coming soon) for more insight on how BDC enables the Data Modeler to contribute to the success of the data and analytics team. 

Data Scientist

Data scientists utilize historical patterns to anticipate future trends, enabling informed business decisions in the present. This role requires access to high-quality data to construct and train models employing statistical analysis and intricate logic.

SAP Business Data Cloud facilitates the acquisition of high-quality data from both SAP and non-SAP sources through SAP Datasphere, SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Databricks, and SAP Snowflake, thereby establishing a comprehensive business data fabric. Additionally, this role leverages the advanced SQL, notebook, and data science capabilities of these systems to disseminate complex models utilizing all pertinent enterprise data.

Visit this blog post for more insight on how BDC enables the Data Scientist to contribute to the success of the data and analytics team.

Developer

Developers bridge the technical gap between business process and backend systems. This role can include any combination of specialty such as mobile applications, web-sites, desktop applications, Api’s, chatbots, or even agentic AI solutions. Incumbents in this role must equally understand logic, design, and data.

SAP Business Data Cloud provide the critical data a developer needs to deploy the best solution to the business. SAP HANA Cloud is a best of bread database for industrial class, high-performance data platforms designed to run business processes in real-time. This role specifically takes advantage of this in-memory, multi-model platform by utilizing every type of data within the organization to provide an experience that is engaging and intuitive. Solutions built using SAP HANA Cloud are also natively part of a largely business data fabric – bidirectionally sharing data so that all aspects of the business can operate in real-time and in unison.

Visit this blog post for more insight on how BDC enables the Developer to contribute to the success of the data and analytics team.

Conclusion

Teamwork makes the AI dream work when everyone shares standards, clear governance, and a common data foundation. With SAP BDC, remove the friction across the data lifecycle for impactful business outcomes.

Kindly join and then bookmark the Data Professionals community for relevant resources, events, and discussions.

entry.title: Limit Item PO: Changes to Expected value: Change indicator not displayed in the Standard PO Print
entry.content:
We are using the ‘Limit’ Item functionality in the purchase order and need to see the changes to the ‘Expected Value’ shown as a change in the Standard PO Print.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Limit Item PO: Changes to Expected value: Change indi...
entry.content:
In order to add the Expected Value in the Purchase Order Output you need to add a new text in the Define Change Texts SSCUI 102271 with a custom ID, then you need to add the EXPECTEDOVERALLLIMITAMOUNT in the Fields Relevant to Printouts of Changes configuration SSCUI 102272 and map to the newly created ID. This will triggered the change with the correct text. See more information on the blog post: How to generate purchase order output on change of a purchase order?.

Thanks,
Diéssica

entry.title: How to trigger the putaway list based on warehouse tasks?
entry.content:
Hi,

The business wants to trigger the putaway list based on warehouse tasks. Could you please help with the required setup for this? I’m unable to find any option to configure this in the Output Parameter Determination.

Thanks.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to trigger the putaway list based on warehouse ta...
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

The putaway list includes the warehouse order and warehouse tasks needed for the putaway process. It also contains the warehouse order and inbound delivery order as the bar code on header level.

The putaway list is relevant for open warehouse orders with warehouse process type S110 (Putaway) or S115 (Putaway from Production), and it's issued when creating tasks in the Change Inbound Deliveries - Deliveries app and the Change Inbound Deliveries - Production app.

The supported channel is printout.

To adapt the layout to your needs, copy the form template EWM_PUTAWAY_LIST_2.

For further details, please check the document below: 

Putaway List | SAP Help Portal.

 

Best regards,

SAP Support.

entry.title: Re: Event Mesh + AMQP: The Retry Trap Nobody Warns You About
entry.content:
Re. Another question for what reason it is better to use JSM in case of Heavy iFlow logic?
How can the type of the queue have an impact on the processing of the iFlow?

Ans.
First reason - decoupling.
You consume from Event Mesh and immediately write to internal JMS queue, then finish the iFlow as COMPLETED. Event Mesh message is acknowledged quickly, so you’re not holding broker resources while doing long processing.

Second reason - better retry control.
Heavy flows tend to hit transient issues. Then exponential backoff matters that is covered only in JMS, not AMQP adapter.

entry.title: Price Amount Recalculation in Peppol E-Invoicing for SAP
entry.content:
Introduction

In the world of electronic invoicing, especially within the Peppol network, precision is paramount. A single misplaced decimal can lead to rejected invoices, delayed payments, and frustrated business partners. One of the frequently discussed topics is the calculation of the PriceAmount field in UBL invoices. This blog post aims to shed light on why SAP's standard solution for Document and Reporting Compliance (e-invoicing) behaves the way it does, and how you can tailor it to your specific business needs.

Scope Note: This blog post covers the SAP S/4HANA on-premise implementation and S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition using BAdI EDOC_ADAPTOR, and assuming the latest version as per 2783795 - Electronic Document Processing for Peppol Countries/Regions: Implementation Overview - SAP for Me is implemented. 
This blog post refers only to eDocuments which were created out of Sales & Distribution Billing documents, as those allow price conditions as allowances or surcharges.
For SAP Public Edition, ABAP Environment, the equivalent BAdI is EDOC_ADAPTOR_CLOUD. The overall approach is similar, but backend APIs to format the amounts in the XML may differ and are not covered here.

As this behavior is cross-country enabled and as the mapping for CrossIndustryInvoice for Germany is implemented on top of the Peppol mapping, this is also valid for Germany / ZUGFeRD process as outlined here.

What is the Issue? The Peppol Rule PEPPOL-EN16931-R120

At the heart of the matter lies a critical Peppol validation rule: PEPPOL-EN16931-R120. This rule mandates that the Invoice line net amount must be consistent with the quantity and price. In simple terms, the following equation must hold true (within a defined rounding tolerance):

LineExtensionAmount = InvoicedQuantity * (PriceAmount / BaseQuantity)

If an invoice line includes discounts or surcharges (AllowanceCharge), the formula expands:

LineExtensionAmount = (InvoicedQuantity * (PriceAmount / BaseQuantity)) - Allowances + Charges

If this mathematical check fails, the Peppol Access Point will reject the invoice. The ultimate goal of the SAP standard solution is to ensure every generated invoice is compliant and successfully transmitted through the network.

Why SAP Cannot Deliver the Perfect Calculation in Every Case

The challenge arises because different businesses represent prices and discounts in different ways. SAP's e-invoicing solution must balance flexibility with the strict need for compliance.

SAP's standard mapping tries to generate a valid invoice that will be accepted by the Peppol Network. It does this by recalculating the PriceAmount if it detects a potential violation of the R120 rule. This often happens in two common scenarios:

  1. Hidden Discounts/Surcharges: Many businesses prefer not to show item-level discounts or surcharges separately in the final XML, at least not all of them. At the same time, they want the PriceAmount to reflect the net price. If the discount/surcharge conditions are not mapped to the <cac:AllowanceCharge> element in the XML, the standard PriceAmount (often derived from a gross price condition) will not match the final LineExtensionAmount. SAP's logic detects this and recalculates the PriceAmount to absorb these "hidden" values.

  2. High-Precision Prices: For low-priced items sold in bulk, kWh sold in Utilities, or items with prices derived from complex calculations, the calculated unit price can have many decimal places (e.g., €7.39933 per piece). The UBL standard allows for this, but historically, currency fields were often handled with only two decimal places. SAP's standard logic scales the price, but keeps two digits, avoiding rounding errors that would violate rule PEPPOL-EN16931-R120. So instead of showing 7.39933 / 1 piece, it might show 7399.33 / 1000 pieces. While mathematically identical, this can be confusing for human readers of the invoice.

This scaling logic is what customers may find "weird". However, it's a safe-by-default mechanism to guarantee compliance.

Tailoring the Calculation with a BAdI Implementation

The good news is that you have full control. Using the Business Add-In (BAdI) EDOC_ADAPTOR, you can implement your own logic to override the standard scaling behavior.

The best place to do this is in the BAdI method IF_EDOC_ADAPTOR~SET_OUTPUT_DATA. This method is called after SAP has performed its standard mapping, giving you the final data structure just before it's converted to XML.

Here is a sample implementation that rescales the price based on a simple rule: if the invoiced quantity is less than 1000, use a BaseQuantity of 1; otherwise, use 1000. This provides a more predictable and readable output, and can be fine-tuned to your needs.

CLASS zcl_edoc_adaptor_scaling IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD if_edoc_adaptor~set_output_data.
    " This BAdI implementation rescales the PriceAmount and BaseQuantity
    " according to a custom logic, overriding the standard SAP scaling.

    FIELD-SYMBOLS:
      <fs_invoice_data>   TYPE any,
      <lt_invoice_line>   TYPE STANDARD TABLE,
      <fs_line>           TYPE any,
      <fs_price>          TYPE any,
      <fs_price_amount>   TYPE any,
      <fs_base_quantity>  TYPE any,
      <fs_inv_quantity>   TYPE any.

    DATA:
      lv_current_price     TYPE p LENGTH 16 DECIMALS 10,
      lv_current_base_qty  TYPE p LENGTH 16 DECIMALS 10,
      lv_invoiced_quantity TYPE fklmg,
      lv_unit_price        TYPE p LENGTH 16 DECIMALS 10,
      lv_new_base_quantity TYPE p LENGTH 16 DECIMALS 10,
      lv_new_price_amount  TYPE p LENGTH 16 DECIMALS 10.

    IF iv_interface_id CS 'UBL_INVOICE_'.
      ASSIGN cs_output_data TO <fs_invoice_data>.
      IF <fs_invoice_data> IS NOT ASSIGNED. RETURN. ENDIF.

      ASSIGN COMPONENT 'INVOICE-INVOICE_LINE' OF STRUCTURE <fs_invoice_data> TO <lt_invoice_line>.
      IF <lt_invoice_line> IS NOT ASSIGNED. RETURN. ENDIF.

      LOOP AT <lt_invoice_line> ASSIGNING <fs_line>.
        " Get current Price, BaseQuantity, and InvoicedQuantity
        ASSIGN COMPONENT 'PRICE' OF STRUCTURE <fs_line> TO <fs_price>.
        ASSIGN COMPONENT 'INVOICED_QUANTITY-BASE-BASE-CONTENT' OF STRUCTURE <fs_line> TO <fs_inv_quantity>.
        IF <fs_price> IS NOT ASSIGNED OR <fs_inv_quantity> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
          CONTINUE.
        ENDIF.

        ASSIGN COMPONENT 'PRICE_AMOUNT-BASE-CONTENT' OF STRUCTURE <fs_price> TO <fs_price_amount>.
        ASSIGN COMPONENT 'BASE_QUANTITY-BASE-BASE-CONTENT' OF STRUCTURE <fs_price> TO <fs_base_quantity>.

        IF <fs_price_amount> IS NOT ASSIGNED OR <fs_base_quantity> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
          CONTINUE.
        ENDIF.
        
        lv_current_price = <fs_price_amount>.
        lv_current_base_qty = <fs_base_quantity>.
        lv_invoiced_quantity = <fs_inv_quantity>.

        IF lv_current_base_qty = 0.
          CONTINUE.
        ENDIF.

        " 1. Calculate the true unit price from the existing values
        lv_unit_price = lv_current_price / lv_current_base_qty.

        " 2. *** CUSTOMER-SPECIFIC SCALING LOGIC STARTS HERE ***
        IF lv_invoiced_quantity < 1000.
          lv_new_base_quantity = 1.
        ELSE.
          lv_new_base_quantity = 1000.
        ENDIF.
        " *** CUSTOMER-SPECIFIC SCALING LOGIC ENDS HERE ***

        " 3. Calculate the new PriceAmount based on the new BaseQuantity
        lv_new_price_amount = lv_unit_price * lv_new_base_quantity.

        " 4. Write the new values back and format for XML output
        <fs_price_amount>  = lv_new_price_amount.
        <fs_base_quantity> = lv_new_base_quantity.

        cl_edoc_map_aif=>format_negative_amount( CHANGING cv_amount = <fs_price_amount> ).
        cl_edoc_map_aif=>format_negative_amount( CHANGING cv_amount = <fs_base_quantity> ).

      ENDLOOP.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

Summary

Navigating Peppol compliance requires a careful balance between standard processes and business-specific needs. While SAP's default behavior for PriceAmount recalculation prioritizes compliance with rule PEPPOL-EN16931-R120 to ensure your invoices are accepted, it may not always align with your desired representation.

Key Takeaways

  • Compliance is King: The primary reason for the recalculation is to comply with PEPPOL-EN16931-R120 and prevent invoice rejection.
  • You Have Choices: You can configure how discounts and surcharges appear. Either show them separately in <cac:AllowanceCharge> elements or absorb them into a net PriceAmount. The latter often triggers the recalculation.
  • You Have Control: The EDOC_ADAPTOR BAdI provides a powerful, upgrade-safe way to implement your own pricing representation logic, overriding the default scaling to produce invoices that are both compliant and easy for your customers to understand.

entry.title: New Learning Journey + Knowledge Badge: Joule & Business AI in SAP CX
entry.content:
On behalf of the Product learning team, I am excited to announce the launch of a new learning journey with a knowledge badge : 
Exploring Joule and Business AI in SAP Customer Experience.

This journey helps SAP CX professionals understand how Joule, SAP’s AI copilot, and SAP Business AI enhance productivity and transform customer experience processes across sales, service, commerce, and marketing. Learners will explore how AI-driven insights streamline decision-making, improve implementation efficiency, and support the evolving role of the next-generation CX consultant.  It ends in a self paced assessment, that helps obtain a knowledge badge.

🌟What This Learning Journey Covers

This learning journey provides a comprehensive introduction to:

  • The role of Joule and Business AI across SAP Customer Experience solutions  
  • How AI capabilities enhance CX business processes end‑to‑end  
  • Key Joule agent use cases supporting sales, service, commerce, and marketing scenarios  
  • How consultants can leverage Joule for faster solution design, configuration, troubleshooting, and advisory work  
  • How embedded AI accelerates implementations and supports the skill set of the Next Generation CX Consultant  

These insights equip learners to explore AI-enabled workflows and elevate their mastery of the SAP CX portfolio.

🎯Who Should Join?

This learning journey is ideal for:

  • SAP CX consultants
  • Solution architects
  • Business users and CX practitioners
  • Anyone preparing to work with AI-driven customer experience capabilities

🧠 How This Learning Journey Helps You

After completing the modules, learners will be able to:

  • Explain how Joule and Business AI enhance customer experience processes
  • Identify how Joule agents assist in real business scenarios
  • Understand how consultants can use AI to accelerate design, configuration, and optimization tasks
  • Navigate AI-driven productivity tools embedded across the SAP CX suite
     

🎓Earn a Knowledge Badge

Upon completing the learning journey, learners become eligible to attempt a Knowledge Badge assessment.

Here’s how it works:

  • You will answer 30 questions in a mix of multiple-choice and multiple-response formats.
  • The exam evaluates your understanding of Joule, Business AI concepts, and the scenarios covered in the learning journey.
  • Successful completion awards you an official SAP Knowledge Badge, which can be showcased on your SAP profile and shared externally.
  • You can attempt the questions any number of times.

This is a great way to validate your understanding and demonstrate your readiness for AI‑powered CX roles.

📚Start Learning Today

The learning journey is completely free and available now on SAP Learning.
Access it directly here:
https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/exploring-joule-and-business-ai-in-sap-customer-experience

entry.title: Business Processes Management Learning Series 2026 (Open to students and professors!)
entry.content:
Business Process Management – Guided Learning from the SAP Learning Hub

This course offers a structured introduction to Business Process Management based on carefully selected chapters from the SAP Learning Hub’s Learning Journeys. The hosts have cheery-picked the most relevant and impactful chapter for you. The selected content focuses on understanding, navigating, and analyzing business processes in an SAP context, combining theoretical foundations with system-oriented perspectives. All chapters are completed by students in a self-paced learning mode, allowing for a high degree of flexibility and individual learning speed.

To complement the self-paced format, the course includes weekly online sessions. Each weekly live session begins with 25 minutes of new content, including a short poll, followed by a 5-minute breakout exercise to apply key concepts. Afterwards, another 15 minutes of new content are delivered, again supported by a quick poll. The session concludes with a 10-minute Q&A covering current and previous topics, as well as brief preparation guidance for the following week, ensuring continuity and structured learning progress.

A Flexible Learning Series– Guided and Hands-On

The program is designed to be manageable alongside your studies or other commitments. In total, it takes about 40 hours. Around 30 hours are dedicated to the self-paced Learning material from the SAP Learning Hub, which you can complete whenever it suits you. The pace is up to you, and you can revisit the content whenever you like.

In addition, there are 11 live Zoom sessions, each lasting one hour (Thursdays, 4:00 pm - 5:00 pm CET) These take place weekly over the course of the program and give you the chance to hear explanations from an BPM and ERP experts, ask questions, and learn from real-world stories. This steady rhythm ensures that learning stays consistent while never becoming overwhelming.

To make the learning even more interactive, you will also get access to live practice system like  SAP S/4HANA , SAP Signavio and SAP LeanIX where you can complete guided exercises and try out processes for yourself. This way, the course connects theory and application in a very concrete way.

Boost Your Skills with Practice, Networking, and Certification

In this learning series, you will gain a solid understanding of Business Process Manangement. You will also meet other participants with similar interests and exchange ideas during the weekly sessions. The interaction with an BPM and ERP experts and your peers makes the learning experience lively and collaborative.

Please note: This program is exclusively available to lecturers and students from officially accredited educational institutions. Registrants need to prove their eligibility by registering with an e-mail address referring to an academic e-mail account!

Overall, the course offers a mixture of knowledge, practice, and community. It is an exciting chance to dive into the world of SAP, learn how companies manage their processes, and develop skills that will stay valuable in the future.

entry.title: Re: Waitlist Notification
entry.content:
Hi @EricGreen ,

Is the setting 'Auto Enroll from Waitlist' enabled at the class's record > Details tab?

  • https://me.sap.com/notes/0003481348
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-learning/managing-sap-successfactors-learning-for-administrators/enabling-waitlists?version=2511
    • Set Auto Enroll from Waitlist to Yes if you want to automatically fill people from the waitlist when a seat opens up in the course.
    • Set Auto Enroll from Waitlist to No if you want to manually control how seats are filled from the waitlist when people withdraw from courses.

Best regards,
